title: [lichen_l2.c:482-565] peer_try_all_pubkeys iteration order is not randomized
description:
Completeness: Side-channel analysis category.

peer_try_all_pubkeys() at lines 482-565 iterates through peer_table in array index order (for size_t i = 0; i < CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS; i++).

The function has a constant-time claim (line 493-502) to prevent timing side-channels. However:
1. Peers are tried in deterministic order (index 0, 1, 2, ...)
2. Even with full iteration, cache timing may differ based on which index matched
3. The peer at index 0 has its verification performed first every time

While the function continues checking all peers after finding a match (constant iteration count), the cache/memory access pattern reveals the matching peer's table position through microarchitectural side channels.

For security-critical deployments, consider randomizing iteration start index (e.g., start = random % count, wrap around) to make position correlation harder.

This is P4 because the existing mitigation (full iteration) is likely sufficient for the threat model.
close_reason: Added FUTURE comment about randomizing iteration start index to mitigate microarchitectural side channels
